license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bdv2i68e
title: Nuclei isolation from mouse lung for single nucleus RNASeq
author: Jeffrey Koenitzer

steps_raw.instruction: Pre-cool all instruments (including centrifuges), buffers, and tubes. Note: All steps are performed On ice or in cold room ( 4 °C ) to minimize RNA degradation.
Remove mouse lung sample from -80 ºC freezer and trim a ~6 mm 3 piece .
Transfer the minced tissue and buffer to a GentleMacs C tube.
Run m_lung_01 program and m_lung_02 program in sequence, stopping the latter after 00:00:20 . Foam after GentleMacs
Reduce foam by centrifugation ( 750 x g, °C, 1 minute ). Foam after centrifugation
Wash strainer with 4 mL, Wash Buffer .
Pass suspension through a 5 µm strainer into 50 ml conical tube.

Count nuclei by hemocytometer and dilute to desired concentration (e.g. 10,000 per µl). Note : it is easier to resuspend in lower volumes and dilute than to concentrate nuclei via further centrifugation. 40x Nuclear suspension with scant debris

Thaw on small plastic weighing boat until able to insert 26G needle to tissue, then inject ice cold 1 mL, Lysis Buffer to ‘inflate’ the tissue. Add the remaining Lysis Buffer and chop to the smallest pieces possible with scissors (60 s).
Close, invert, and transfer directly to MACS Tissue dissociator.
Using a wide bore tip, pipette up and down to recover any pelleted material and pass lysate through 40 µm filter to 50 ml conical tube.
